groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Business name, review platform, review text, sentiment, goal, destination, verified facts, reviewer name, and output language.

groups.title: Review Context & Resolution
groups.description: Issue category, case status, approved resolution, private contact method, and boundaries.

groups.title: Brand & Tone
groups.description: Response tone, brand voice, sign-off, and words or phrases to avoid.

groups.title: Output Settings
groups.description: Response length, number of variants, and optional internal risk note.

variables.label: Business or brand name
variables.hint: Identifies the business responding to the review.

help.what: Provide the business or brand name for this review response.
help.why: The model must know which business is speaking and how to frame the response.
help.avoid: Avoid invented brand names, unrelated subsidiaries, or legal entity details that are not meant to appear in the response.

variables.label: Review platform
variables.hint: Helps adapt the response to the expected platform style.

help.what: Optional context for the review platform field.
help.why: Public review platforms differ in tone, context, and practical response conventions.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Customer review
variables.placeholder: Paste the complete customer review here.
variables.hint: Include the original wording so the response can acknowledge specific details.

help.what: Provide the customer review for this review response.
help.why: The review itself is the primary source for sentiment, concerns, and relevant details.
help.example: Good example: The hygienist was kind, but I waited 40 minutes past my appointment time.
help.avoid: Avoid summarizing the review from memory, omitting key complaints, or adding facts the reviewer did not write.

variables.label: Review sentiment
variables.hint: Choose a sentiment only when automatic detection may be misleading.

help.what: Provide the review sentiment for this review response.
help.why: Sentiment materially changes the acknowledgment, apology level, and response structure.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing context, unsupported facts, or conflicting goals that would make the response unsafe.

variables.label: Primary response goal
variables.hint: Defines what the response should achieve without requiring marketing terminology.

help.what: Provide the primary response goal for this review response.
help.why: A thank-you response, factual correction, and complaint recovery require different approaches.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing context, unsupported facts, or conflicting goals that would make the response unsafe.

variables.label: Response destination
variables.placeholder: Choose where the response will appear
variables.hint: Select whether the output is public, private, or includes both versions.

help.what: Optional context for the response destination field.
help.why: Public and private responses require different privacy and detail levels.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Verified facts
variables.placeholder: Add confirmed facts, records, dates, actions, or relevant context.
variables.hint: Only include information the business can safely and accurately state.

help.what: Optional context for the verified facts field.
help.why: Verified facts are necessary when clarifying events or correcting misinformation.
help.example: Good example: The appointment ran late because an emergency visit extended the previous slot.
help.avoid: Avoid assumptions, internal guesses, unsupported records-checked claims, or private details that should not appear publicly.

variables.label: Reviewer name
variables.hint: Used only when a personal greeting feels natural and privacy-safe.

help.what: Optional context for the reviewer name field.
help.why: A supplied name can make the response more personal without requiring it.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Output language
variables.hint: Sets the language of the complete response package.

help.what: Provide the output language for this review response.
help.why: The response language cannot always be reliably inferred from the review.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing context, unsupported facts, or conflicting goals that would make the response unsafe.

variables.label: Issue category
variables.hint: Helps the model emphasize the right concern and apply appropriate caution.

help.what: Optional context for the issue category field.
help.why: Different complaint categories require different acknowledgment and risk handling.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Current case status
variables.hint: Prevents the response from implying progress or resolution that has not occurred.

help.what: Optional context for the current case status field.
help.why: Case status determines which operational statements can be made truthfully.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Approved resolution details
variables.placeholder: Describe what was done or what action is currently approved.
variables.hint: Add only resolution details that may be communicated to the reviewer.

help.what: Optional context for the approved resolution details field.
help.why: It prevents the model from inventing refunds, replacements, investigations, or outcomes.
help.example: Good example: The office manager may review the scheduling issue and follow up privately.
help.avoid: Avoid invented refunds, replacements, investigations, callbacks, deadlines, compensation, or outcomes.

variables.label: Private contact method
variables.placeholder: Example: Email support@example.com with the order number.
variables.hint: Provide the approved channel and any safe instructions for private follow-up.

help.what: Optional context for the private contact method field.
help.why: The model must not invent contact information or unsupported escalation paths.
help.example: Good example: Email support@example.com with the appointment date and preferred contact method.
help.avoid: Avoid fake email addresses, unsupported escalation routes, public requests for sensitive data, or private details in a public reply.

variables.label: Boundaries and restrictions
variables.placeholder: Add relevant policies, legal boundaries, or details that must remain private.
variables.hint: Combine policy limits, prohibited promises, and sensitive topics in one field.

help.what: Optional context for the boundaries and restrictions field.
help.why: It gives the model necessary operational limits without creating multiple compliance fields.
help.example: Good example: Do not discuss medical details publicly or promise compensation.
help.avoid: Avoid vague warnings that do not explain what the response must not say or promise.

variables.label: Response tone
variables.hint: Choose a tone only when the automatic professional style is not sufficient.

help.what: Optional context for the response tone field.
help.why: Tone changes how acknowledgment, apology, and boundaries are expressed.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Example: Friendly, practical, calm, and never overly corporate.
variables.hint: Describe only the voice qualities that should meaningfully affect the response.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: It allows the response to sound consistent with the business rather than generic.
help.example: Good example: Friendly, practical, calm, and never overly corporate.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Preferred sign-off
variables.hint: Leave blank when the platform does not require a closing signature.

help.what: Optional context for the preferred sign-off field.
help.why: Some businesses require a consistent team or representative signature.
help.example: Good example: The Northside Support Team
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Words or phrases to avoid
variables.placeholder: Example: valued customer, inconvenience caused, company policy
variables.hint: List wording that conflicts with the brand or situation.

help.what: Optional context for the words or phrases to avoid field.
help.why: Specific prohibited wording cannot always be inferred from general tone instructions.
help.example: Good example: valued customer, inconvenience caused, company policy
help.avoid: Avoid listing every disliked style preference when only a few phrases are truly prohibited.

variables.label: Response length
variables.hint: Controls brevity while allowing the model to preserve essential information.

help.what: Optional context for the response length field.
help.why: Businesses may need anything from a one-sentence acknowledgment to a fuller recovery response.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate one final response or up to three meaningfully different versions.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: Some users want a final answer, while others need several phrasing options for approval.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.

variables.label: Include internal risk note
variables.placeholder: Select whether to include a private note
variables.hint: Adds a private note about missing facts, privacy risks, or escalation needs.

help.what: Optional context for the include internal risk note field.
help.why: Sensitive reviews may require a concise warning without placing internal analysis in the public reply.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, unsupported promises, private customer data, accusations, fake urgency, or wording that pressures review changes.
